GEN 1:1.text: YLT (Young's Literal Translation, 1862) aims for extreme literalness, preserving Hebrew and Greek word order and grammar.
'In the beginning of God's preparing' - YLT uses 'preparing' (Hebrew 'bara') instead of 'creating' to preserve the Hebrew sense of shaping.
YLT preserves Hebrew verb tenses, often using present tense where other translations use past.

GEN 1:2.text: 'the earth hath existed waste and void' - YLT uses 'hath existed' to preserve Hebrew verb forms.
'the Spirit of God fluttering' - Hebrew 'merachephet', YLT uses 'fluttering' to capture the bird-like imagery.

GEN 1:26.text: 'Make we man in our image' - YLT preserves Hebrew imperative 'na'aseh' (let us make) with 'Make we'.
'as our likeness' - Hebrew 'kidmutenu', YLT uses 'as' to preserve the preposition.

GEN 2:7.text: ' Jehovah God formeth the man' - YLT uses 'Jehovah' for YHWH and 'formeth' (present tense) for Hebrew 'vayyitzer'.
'breath of lives' - Hebrew 'nishmat chayyim', YLT preserves plural 'lives' (not 'life').
'the man becometh a living creature' - Hebrew 'l'nephesh chayah', YLT uses 'creature' not 'soul'.

GEN 3:15.text: 'he doth bruise thee - the head' - YLT preserves Hebrew word order: verb-object-subject.
'thou dost bruise him - the heel' - YLT shows the parallel structure of the Hebrew poetry.

EXO 3:14.text: 'I AM THAT WHICH I AM' - Hebrew 'Ehyeh asher ehyeh', YLT uses 'which' to preserve the relative pronoun.
'I AM hath sent me unto you' - YLT preserves 'I AM' (Ehyeh) as the divine name.

PSA 23:1.text: 'Jehovah is my shepherd' - YLT consistently uses 'Jehovah' for the divine name YHWH.
'I do not lack' - Hebrew 'lo echsar', YLT uses present tense 'do not lack' rather than future 'shall not want'.

PSA 23:4.text: 'the valley of the shadow of death' - Hebrew 'gey tsalmavet', YLT preserves literal 'shadow of death'.
'Thy rod and thy staff - they comfort me' - YLT adds dash for Hebrew emphasis.

ISA 7:14.text: 'the virgin is conceiving' - Hebrew 'harah ha'almah', YLT uses present participle 'is conceiving'.
'Immanuel' - YLT preserves Hebrew name without translation.

ISA 9:6.text: 'Wonder, Counsellor, Mighty God' - Hebrew 'pele yoetz el gibbor', YLT preserves Hebrew word order.
'Father of Eternity' - Hebrew 'avi ad', YLT translates literally rather than 'Everlasting Father'.

MAT 1:23.text: 'Lo, the virgin shall be with child' - Greek 'idou he parthenos', YLT uses 'Lo' for 'idou' (behold).
'they shall call his name Emmanuel' - YLT preserves Hebrew name, explaining 'is, being interpreted, With us [is] God'.

MAT 5:3.text: 'Happy the poor in spirit' - Greek 'makarioi hoi ptochoi', YLT uses 'Happy' not 'Blessed' for 'makarioi'.
'because theirs is the reign of the heavens' - YLT uses 'reign' (basileia) not 'kingdom'.

MAT 6:33.text: 'seek ye first the reign of God' - YLT uses 'reign' (basileia) consistently throughout.
'and all these things shall be added to you' - YLT preserves future tense 'shall be added'.

JHN 1:1.text: 'In the beginning was the Word' - Greek 'en arche en ho logos', YLT preserves Greek word order.
'and the Word was with God' - Greek 'kai ho logos en pros ton theon', YLT preserves 'with' (pros).
'and the Word was God' - Greek 'kai theos en ho logos', YLT preserves word order emphasizing 'God'.

JHN 3:16.text: 'for God did so love the world' - Greek 'houtos gar egapesen', YLT uses 'did so love' for emphatic 'houtos'.
'that His Son - the only begotten - He gave' - YLT uses dashes to preserve Greek apposition.

JHN 14:6.text: 'I am the way, and the truth, and the life' - Greek 'ego eimi he hodos kai he aletheia kai he zoe', YLT preserves articles.
'no one doth come unto the Father, but through me' - YLT uses 'doth come' for Greek present tense.

ROM 3:23.text: 'all did sin' - Greek 'pantes hemarton', YLT uses past tense 'did sin' for aorist.
'and are come short of the glory of God' - Greek 'hysterountai', YLT uses 'are come short' for present tense.

ROM 5:8.text: 'God doth commend His own love to us' - Greek 'sunistesin', YLT uses 'doth commend' for present tense.
'in that, being yet sinners, Christ for us died' - YLT preserves Greek word order with participles.

ROM 6:23.text: 'the wages of the sin [is] death' - YLT adds brackets for implied 'is' in Greek.
'and the gift of God [is] life age-during' - YLT uses 'age-during' (aionios) not 'eternal'.

ROM 8:28.text: 'we have known that to those loving God all things do work together for good' - YLT uses 'do work' for present tense.
'to those who are called according to purpose' - Greek 'kletous ousin', YLT uses 'are called' for present participle.

EPH 2:8.text: 'by the grace ye have been saved, through faith' - Greek 'te gar chariti este sesosmenoi', YLT uses perfect tense 'have been saved'.
'and this not of you - of God the gift' - YLT uses dashes for Greek emphasis.

PHP 4:13.text: 'for all things I have strength, in Christ's strengthening me' - Greek 'panta ischyo en to endynamounti me', YLT preserves structure.

HEB 4:12.text: 'the reckoning of God is living, and working' - Greek 'ho logos tou theou zon kai energes', YLT uses 'reckoning' for 'logos'.
'sharper than any two-mouthed sword' - Greek 'machaira distomos', YLT uses literal 'two-mouthed'.

1JN 1:9.text: 'if we may confess our sins' - Greek 'ean homologomen', YLT uses 'may confess' for subjunctive mood.
'faithful He is and righteous' - Greek 'pistos estin kai dikaios', YLT inverts for emphasis.
